divergences.decision: Operator-facing dscli is privileged infrastructure tooling and security is an explicit quality requirement in the mental model (PRD §2.2, PRD §3, arc42 §7 and §10.1).
divergences.detail: The PR adds cli/net_check.py as a dscli reachability helper, but ping_worker interpolates the caller-controlled host into a shell command and runs it with shell=True. For an operator/SRE CLI used around cluster bring-up and deployment, that makes host input executable shell syntax instead of a network target, diverging from the MM's infrastructure/security posture. Nearby CLI helpers use argv-list subprocess execution and validate shell-sensitive inputs, so the new helper also departs from the established safe CLI convention.
divergences.fix: Avoid shell=True and pass argv as a list, e.g. subprocess.run(["ping", "-c", str(count), host], ...), while validating host/count with existing CLI validation helpers such as IP/address validation and numeric bounds.
